frameworks.name: recurrent network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Cortical circuits as recurrent networks whose integrating modes shape information flow.

frameworks.name: excitatory-inhibitory connectivity
frameworks.one_line: E-I synaptic changes among feature-tuned neurons reshape circuit dynamics with learning.

core_question: How does learning reshape recurrent cortical dynamics to better integrate task-relevant sensory input?
key_assumptions: sensory information is read out from recurrent network output
integrating modes of circuit dynamics gate information transmission
feedforward input alignment with these modes determines coding fidelity
manipulations: alignment between feedforward input and integrating circuit modes (theory)
visual discrimination learning over training in mice
key_findings: analytical link between mode alignment and information available in network output
slow network dynamics in visual cortex realigned with learning to better integrate task-relevant input
realignment explained by changes in E-I connectivity among neurons tuned to relevant features
interpretations: learning tunes temporal dynamics of cortical circuits to optimally integrate relevant sensory input
